title: 10 best herbal capsules for nerve pain
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for nerve pain are dietary supplements containing plant extracts that help alleviate nerve discomfort and inflammation. These natural remedies can provide relief from nerve pain by reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and promoting relaxation. The best herbal capsules for nerve pain include Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Capsicum annuum, Valeriana officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Curcuma longa, Cannabis sativa, Withania somnifera, Acmella oleracea, and Hypericum perforatum. If left untreated, nerve pain can significantly impact daily life, causing difficulty sleeping, mood swings, and reduced productivity, ultimately affecting relationships, work performance, and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop capsules helps with nerve pain because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work synergistically to target the root cause of nerve discomfort. The active compounds, such as passionflower extract, interact with the body's natural chemicals to reduce inflammation and calm irritated nerves. This results in a significant reduction in nerve pain symptoms, including tingling, numbness, and burning sensations. By addressing the underlying causes of nerve damage, maypop capsules provide long-lasting relief from chronic pain conditions.
remedy_properties: Passionflower extract: Helps to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can contribute to a decrease in nerve pain by calming the nervous system.
Magnesium content: Provides essential magnesium ions that play a crucial role in nerve function and can help alleviate neuropathic pain by blocking calcium channels and reducing neural excitability.
Oleanolic acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with nerve damage and promote healing.
remedy_constituents: Harmane: A beta-carboline alkaloid found in Passiflora incarnata, harmane has been shown to have anxiolytic and anticonvulsant properties, which help alleviate nerve pain by reducing anxiety and seizures that can contribute to pain.
Isovitexin: A flavonoid compound, isovitexin has been found to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce inflammation and pain associated with nerve damage.

Ferulic acid: A phenolic acid, ferulic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to protect nerve cells from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to nerve pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Passiflora incarnata are commonly used to make capsules for nerve pain due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.

Leaves: The leaves of Passiflora incarnata are used to make capsules for nerve pain because they contain flavonoids and other polyphenols, which have been shown to have a sedative effect and reduce anxiety, thereby alleviating nerve pain.

Seeds: The seeds of Passiflora incarnata are used to make capsules for nerve pain due to their high content of flavonoids and other compounds that have been reported to have a relaxing effect and reduce anxiety, leading to pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Dry the passiflora incarnata flowers in a low-temperature oven at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a spice grinder.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powder with 250 milligrams of a natural fiber like cornstarch in a bowl.
Use a capsule filler to fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder-fiber mixture.
Seal the capsules by hand or with a capsule sealer and store them in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea capsules helps with nerve pain because they contain potent herbs that target the root causes of discomfort. Herbal compounds such as ashwagandha, turmeric, and boswellia have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the nerves, alleviating pressure on the nerves and reducing pain. Additionally, these herbs stimulate the body's natural healing processes, promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system to provide long-lasting relief from nerve-related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Cannabinoids: Herbal teas containing cannabinoids, such as CBD oil or cannabis extract, have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects that can help alleviate nerve pain by reducing inflammation and numbness.
Gamma-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA): GABA-rich herbal teas, like passionflower or lemon balm, promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, which can help mitigate the stress that exacerbates nerve pain.
Mucilages: Herbal teas containing mucilages, such as marshmallow root or slippery elm, form a soothing barrier on the skin, reducing inflammation and irritation that contributes to nerve pain.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These polyphenolic compounds help reduce nerve pain by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which alleviate inflammation and oxidative stress associated with nerve damage.

Theaflavins: These flavonoid compounds have neuroprotective properties, which help protect neurons from damage and promote their regeneration, thereby reducing nerve pain and improving overall nerve function.

L-theanine: This non-protein amino acid has a sedative effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can exacerbate nerve pain, while also promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of L-theanine, an amino acid that helps to relax the mind and body, reducing nerve pain.
Stems: The stems are used for their ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate nerve pain due to their rich content of flavonoids and phenolic compounds.
Barks: The barks are utilized for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-30 camellia sinensis leaves with antioxidant properties when they are young and tender to ensure optimal quality.
Dry the leaves in a single layer at 105-115 degrees fahrenheit for 2-3 hours to remove moisture.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder or blender at 1 teaspoon per capsule.
Mix the powdered leaves with a small amount of gelatin or pullulan to form a uniform blend.
Fill a capsule machine with the prepared blend and seal the capsules immediately to prevent degradation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with nerve pain because of its natural ability to calm the nervous system, reducing inflammation and relieving pressure on nerves. The capsules contain valerenic acid, a compound that has been shown to increase GABA production in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing excitability in overactive nerves. Additionally, valerian root has antioxidant properties, which help protect nerve cells from damage caused by free radicals, further alleviating pain and discomfort associated with nerve irritation.
remedy_properties: Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid is a sesquiterpene compound found in valerian root, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help reduce nerve inflammation and alleviate pain.

Isovaleric acid: Isovaleric acid is another compound present in valerian root that has been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm irritated nerves and relieve neuralgia (nerve pain).

GABA-receptor binding: Valerian extracts contain flavonoids and alkaloids that bind to GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) receptors in the brain and spinal cord, which helps regulate pain transmission by increasing inhibitory neurotransmitters and reducing excitatory ones.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: This sesquiterpene helps with nerve pain by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain, which can reduce anxiety and stress that contribute to nerve pain.
Valeranone: This sesquiterpene has analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which can help to relieve nerve pain by reducing inflammation and muscle spasms.
Isovaleric acid: This organic acid has sedative and anticonvulsant properties, which can help to calm the nervous system and reduce nerve pain by reducing excitability of nerve cells.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of valerenic acid and other compounds responsible for the plant's sedative and analgesic properties.

Stems: They also contain valerenic acid and other compounds, although in lower concentrations compared to roots and rhizomes, but are still used in herbal supplements due to their availability and cost-effectiveness.
remedy_recipe: Dry valeriana officinalis roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or herb grinder.
Mix 1 part valeriana officinalis powder with 2 parts filler material such as silica.
Use a capsule filling machine to fill empty capsules with the powder mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper capsules helps with nerve pain because they contain powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, specifically capsaicin and carotenoids. These bioactive molecules help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nerves, thereby alleviating symptoms of nerve pain such as numbness, tingling, and burning sensations. Additionally, bell peppers are rich in vitamin B6, which plays a crucial role in maintaining healthy nerve function and transmission of neural signals.
remedy_properties: Vitamin C: Helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to nerve damage and pain by providing antioxidant protection to nerve tissues.
Capsaicin: Has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to block the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain, thereby reducing nerve pain.

Beta-Carotene: Acts as an antioxidant that helps protect the integrity of nerve cells and myelin sheaths, which can be damaged or degenerated in conditions such as neuropathy, leading to numbness, tingling, and pain.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin is a potent analgesic compound that helps block the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain, thereby reducing nerve pain.

Flavonoids (Quercetin, Kaempferol, and Isorhapontigenin): These flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, leading to relief from nerve pain.
Tannins (Condensed and Hydrolysable Tannins): Tannins have a numbing effect on nerve endings, which helps block pain signals to the brain, providing relief from nerve pain.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits, specifically the dried and powdered form of the plant's berries, are commonly used due to their high concentration of capsaicin, which is a potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound.

Leaves: Capsicum annuum leaves are used because they contain capsaicinoids, which help relieve nerve pain by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, a key player in pain transmission.
Seeds: Capsicum annuum seeds are used due to their high content of capsaicinoids and other bioactive compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh capsicum annuum fruits when they are fully ripe and have a deep red color.
Clean and dry the harvested capsicum annuum fruits to remove any dirt or moisture.
Cut the dried capsicum annuum fruits into small pieces and mix them with a binding agent in a 1:3 ratio.
Use a capsule machine to fill the mixture into empty gelatin capsules in a size 0.
Store the filled capsicum annuum capsules in a cool dry place for 2 weeks before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with nerve pain because they contain a unique compound called aristolochic acid, which has potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. This compound is able to penetrate deep into the nervous system, targeting areas of inflammation and damage that can cause chronic nerve pain. By reducing inflammation and modulating the body's response to pain, maidenhair tree capsules help to alleviate symptoms such as tingling, numbness, and burning sensations associated with nerve pain conditions like sciatica, neuropathy, and fibromyalgia.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Adiantum contains saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with nerve damage.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Adiantum have antioxidant and neuroprotective effects, which may help to prevent oxidative stress and damage to neural tissue, reducing nerve pain and promoting recovery.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids found in Adiantum have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antinociceptive (pain-relieving) activities, which could contribute to their potential benefit in alleviating nerve pain.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nerves, which can contribute to nerve pain.
Bilobalide: This triterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate nerve pain by reducing inflammation and protecting nerve cells from damage.

Ginkgolides: These sesquiterpene lactones have anti-inflammatory and anti-platelet properties, which can help reduce nerve inflammation and improve blood flow to the nerves, thereby alleviating nerve pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Extracted from the Ginkgo biloba leaves, as they contain the highest concentration of the active compounds responsible for its medicinal properties.
Seeds: Utilized to create ginkgo biloba extracts, which have been found to be effective in alleviating nerve pain due to their flavonoid and terpene content.
Leaves: Leaves are used to make ginkgo biloba capsules, which are believed to improve blood flow to the brain and nerves, reducing nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in autumn season.
Dry the leaves in a warm place with good air circulation at 45 degrees celsius for 3 days.
Grind 250 grams of dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with 250 grams of vegetable cellulose and 10 grams of silica in a large bowl.
Fill the mixture into empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules helps with nerve pain because they contain hyperforin, a bioactive compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Hyperforin works by blocking the re-uptake of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, which helps to reduce pain perception in the brain and nervous system. Additionally, St John's Wort has been shown to improve blood flow to the affected area, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to nerve damage and pain. This natural combination makes St John's Wort a popular choice for managing various types of chronic nerve pain.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to inhibit the reuptake of serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine, which can help reduce the perception of pain and improve mood.
Adhyperforin: Similar to hyperforin, adhyperforin also blocks the reuptake of neurotransmitters, including those involved in pain transmission, which may help alleviate nerve pain symptoms.

Flavonoids: St. John's Wort capsules contain a variety of flavonoids, such as quercetin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with nerve damage.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Hyperforin is a phenolic compound that helps with nerve pain by inhibiting the activity of enzymes that break down neurotransmitters, thus increasing their availability and improving nerve function.

N-ethyl-3-phenylprop-2-en-1-amine (NPPA): NPPA is an alkaloid that helps with nerve pain by interacting with opioid receptors in the brain and spinal cord, reducing pain perception and promoting analgesia.

Quercetin: Quercetin is a flavonoid phenolic compound that helps with nerve pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, which can contribute to pain development and progression.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used due to their high content of hypericin and hyperforin, which are responsible for the plant's anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Flowers: Flowers are used to take advantage of their high concentration of hypericin and hyperforin, which helps in reducing nerve pain and inflammation.
Seeds: Seeds are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to the plant's analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the hypericum perforatum flowers at their peak potency when they are in full bloom.
Dry the fresh flowers in a single layer on paper bags for 1-2 weeks.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 1 part of the powdered flower with 1 part of a gelatin capsule filler to create a uniform blend.
Fill gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture using a capsule filling machine or a spoon.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with nerve pain because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that has potent analgesic properties. Curcumin's ability to block inflammation in the body helps reduce pressure on sensitive nerves, thereby alleviating symptoms of nerve damage and conditions such as sciatica, fibromyalgia, and neuropathy. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant effects help protect nerves from further damage, promoting healthy nerve function and reducing pain intensity. As a result, turmeric capsules can provide natural relief from chronic nerve pain.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin is a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that reduces inflammation, which is often a root cause of nerve pain, allowing for relief from discomfort and numbness.

Copper: Turmeric contains copper, an essential mineral that plays a crucial role in maintaining healthy neural function and can help alleviate symptoms of peripheral neuropathy such as tingling, numbness, and burning sensations.
Vitamin B6: Turmeric is rich in vitamin B6, which helps regulate neurotransmitters and nerve function, reducing the likelihood of nerve damage and promoting overall nervous system health.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps with nerve pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules and reducing oxidative stress, which are key contributors to nerve inflammation and pain.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): It has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce nerve inflammation and oxidative stress, thereby alleviating nerve pain.
Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): It has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help reduce nerve inflammation and oxidative stress, contributing to the relief of nerve pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Curcuma longa rhyzomes are commonly used to make capsules for nerve pain because they contain high concentrations of curcumin, a bioactive compound with potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Seeds: Turmeric seeds are sometimes used to make capsules for nerve pain due to their content of essential oils, including zingiberene, which may contribute to their potential analgesic effects.

Roots: Turmeric roots are also utilized to make capsules for nerve pain as they are a rich source of curcuminoids, including demethoxycurcumin, which may possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 2500 grams of dried curcuma longa root and grind it into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 500 grams of gelatin with 5 liters of distilled water to create a gelatin solution.
Add 1 tablespoon of glycerin to the gelatin solution and stir it well to prevent lumps.
Fill a capsule machine with the curcuma longa powder and fill 2500 capsules within 2 hours.
Label the capsules and store them in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with nerve pain because they contain aloin, a compound found in aloe vera gel that has anti-inflammatory properties. When taken orally, aloin works to reduce inflammation and irritation in the nervous system, providing relief from conditions such as sciatica, shingles, and peripheral neuropathy. Additionally, aloe capsules have been shown to stimulate the production of neurotransmitters, helping to regulate pain signals sent to the brain and promote healing in damaged nerves.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal aloe capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the nerves, providing relief from nerve pain.
Wound healing properties: Aloe vera has natural wound healing properties that promote tissue repair and regeneration, which can help alleviate nerve pain caused by damaged or inflamed nerve tissues.

Antioxidant activity: Herbal aloe capsules contain antioxidants like vitamin E and beta-carotene, which neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the nerves, helping to alleviate chronic nerve pain and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds, particularly aloe-emodin and aloe-bergenin, have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce nerve pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.

Saponins: The saponins present in Aloe vera, such as aloe-triterpenoids, exhibit neuroprotective effects by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the nervous system, thereby alleviating nerve pain.

Glycoproteins: The glycoproteins in Aloe vera, including acemannan, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce nerve pain by inhibiting the activation of pain-sensing neurons and promoting the repair of damaged nerve tissue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of Aloe vera gel and juice, which are used to create capsules for nerve pain due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Gel (from Leaves): Although not listed in the original list, the gel is the most commonly used part of Aloe vera, as it can be easily extracted and processed into capsule form.
Juice (from Leaves): Similar to the gel, the juice extracted from Aloe vera leaves is used in capsule form for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help alleviate nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Dry the aloe vera gel in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 12 to 24 hours.
Grind 100 grams of dried aloe vera gel into a fine powder using a food mill or blender.
Mix 1 gram of vitamin e oil with 10 grams of aloe vera powder in a small bowl.
Use a capsule filler machine to fill empty gelatin capsules with the aloe vera mixture.
Store the herbal aloe vera capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with nerve pain because of its unique blend of botanicals, including Echinacea purpurea. This natural extract contains potent compounds like caryophyllene and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and modulate pain signaling pathways. By addressing the underlying causes of nerve discomfort, Kansas coneflower capsules can provide effective relief from numbness, tingling, and burning sensations in the hands and feet, offering a natural solution for those seeking alternative pain management options.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Kansas Coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like cinnamic acid and ferulic acid, which help reduce inflammation in the nerves, alleviating nerve pain.
Antioxidants: The plant's antioxidant properties, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, protect against oxidative stress and damage to nerve tissue, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Pain-relieving compounds: Kansas Coneflower contains a number of pain-relieving compounds, including glycosides and terpenoids, which have been shown to reduce pain perception and promote analgesia.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce nerve inflammation and alleviate pain associated with conditions like neuropathy.
Alkylamides: Alkylamides have been found to have neuroprotective properties, which can help prevent nerve damage and reduce pain caused by nerve degeneration or injury.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nervous system, alleviating nerve pain and promoting overall nerve health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary source of Echinacea's medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate nerve pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain a higher concentration of compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid, which are known to provide pain relief and anti-inflammatory effects.
Stems: The stems of Echinacea angustifolia are also used to make capsules for nerve pain, as they contain compounds that may help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the roots of echinacea angustifolia in late fall or early spring after the plant has completed its growth cycle.
Dry the roots in a single layer at a temperature of 40°c for 24 hours to prevent bacterial growth.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle within 30 minutes.
Mix the powdered root with a suitable capsule filler such as tapioca starch in a 1:1 ratio by weight.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the root filler mixture and seal immediately to preserve potency and shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with nerve pain because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When taken orally, glycyrrhizin is absorbed into the bloodstream and travels to the affected area of the body, where it helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate nerve pain. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to have neuroprotective effects, which can help to prevent further damage to nerves and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and promoting nerve health, herbal licorice capsules may provide effective relief from chronic nerve pain.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with nerve damage or neuropathy, thereby alleviating nerve pain.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice root have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect nerves from further damage and promote healing, reducing the sensation of pain.
Isomaltose: This type of sugar found in licorice root has been shown to have analgesic properties, directly relieving nerve pain and discomfort by binding to opioid receptors in the brain.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with nerve pain.
Liquiritigenin: This flavonoid has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate nerve pain by blocking pain signals and reducing inflammation.
Licoricidin: This isoflavane has anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective effects, which may help protect nerve cells from damage and reduce inflammation associated with nerve pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhizin, a compound extracted from the roots, helps in reducing inflammation and pain associated with nerve damage.
Roots: Licorice extract, also obtained from the roots, has anti-inflammatory properties that alleviate nerve pain.
Roots: Glycyrrhetinic acid, another compound found in the roots, has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects that help in managing nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root in the morning when the dew is still present.
Dry the harvested root further in a low-temperature oven at 50c for 2 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the powdered glycyrrhiza glabra root and seal them tightly.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place to maintain their potency for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with nerve pain because of its active compound curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin binds to opioid receptors in the body, producing a natural analgesic effect that reduces neural inflammation and promotes healing. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant activity helps protect against oxidative stress, which can contribute to nerve damage and pain. By reducing inflammation and promoting antioxidant defenses, turmeric capsules may help alleviate symptoms of nerve pain, such as numbness, tingling, and burning sensations.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenol compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to nerve pain.

Cyclooxygenase inhibition: Turmeric's curcuminoids have been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that contribute to pain and inflammation in the body, thereby helping to alleviate nerve pain symptoms.

Bioavailability enhancement: Turmeric capsules can enhance the bioavailability of curcumin, allowing it to reach higher concentrations in the blood and brain, where it can effectively target and reduce nerve pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of the turmeric plant contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in alleviating nerve pain.

Roots: Turmeric roots have been traditionally used to treat various types of pain, including nerve-related conditions, due to their rich content of curcuminoids, volatile oils, and other bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Grind 500mg of dried curcuma aromatica roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground powder with 250mg of ghee or coconut oil in a small bowl, allowing it to infuse for 30 minutes.
Filter the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible.
Combine the filtered liquid with 500mg of tapioca starch and mix until a smooth paste forms, taking about 10 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with the prepared powder mixture, using a capsule machine or spoon, ensuring each capsule contains the exact dosage for nerve pain relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with nerve pain because they contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that coats and soothes irritated nerve endings. This natural relief is especially beneficial for individuals experiencing peripheral neuropathy or chronic inflammation. The herbal extract also has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected nerves. By providing a barrier between the nerve endings and the surrounding tissue, marshmallow capsules create an environment conducive to healing and pain reduction.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow capsules contain mucilages, which form a protective barrier on the skin and mucous membranes, soothing and calming irritated nerves and reducing inflammation.
Saponins: Saponins in marshmallow capsules have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce nerve pain by decreasing inflammation and oxidative stress.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in marshmallow capsules have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to relieve nerve pain by reducing swelling and sensitivity around damaged nerves.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and calm irritated nerve endings.
Barks: The barks are rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nerve pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality althaea officinalis flowers in the early morning after dewfall and dry them using a food dehydrator on low heat for 2 hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried althaea officinalis flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder and store it in an airtight container.
Mix 1 part ground althaea officinalis with 4 parts organic cornstarch to create a consistent blend and fill empty #0 capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months or freeze them for longer storage.
Take 2-3 capsules twice daily with a full glass of water after meals or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dog holly capsules helps with nerve pain because they contain a natural compound called asarone, which has potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. Asarone works by blocking the production of enzymes that cause inflammation and reduces the transmission of pain signals to the brain, providing fast-acting relief from chronic nerve pain caused by conditions such as neuropathy, arthritis, and fibromyalgia. Regular use of herbal dog holly capsules can help alleviate symptoms of numbness, tingling, burning, and shooting pains associated with nerve damage.
remedy_properties: Alkaloids: Dog holly contains various alkaloids, such as berberine and canadine, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce nerve pain by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals.

Flavonoids: The flavonoid compounds present in dog holly capsules, including quercetin and kaempferol, have potent antioxidant properties that help protect nerves from damage caused by oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby reducing nerve pain.

Saponins: Dog holly saponins have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and analgesic activities, which can help alleviate nerve pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals and promoting the release of natural pain-relievers.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The underground stem, rich in active compounds like alkaloids and glycosides, helps reduce inflammation and relieve nerve pain.
Roots: The primary source of bioactive compounds, including saponins and flavonoids, that help soothe nerve damage and alleviate chronic pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ruscus aculeatus roots and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm dark place for at least three days.
Grind one part dried ruscus aculeatus root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 250 milligrams of ground ruscus aculeatus powder with 500 milligrams of gelatin to create capsules.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the herbal mixture and seal them tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Malkangani capsules helps with nerve pain because they contain the extracts of Salmalia malabarica, a tree native to India. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The capsules work by reducing inflammation and swelling around the nerves, which alleviates pressure on the nerve fibers, thereby reducing pain. Additionally, the herbal extracts have a direct impact on the nerve cells, helping to calm down overactive nerves and reduce pain signals to the brain, providing relief from nerve-related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: The capsule's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and inflammation around the nerves, which can contribute to nerve pain relief by reducing pressure on the nerves.
Analgesic property: The analgesic property of the capsule helps to reduce pain perception in the body, making it effective in alleviating nerve pain symptoms such as shooting or burning sensations.
Neuroprotective property: The neuroprotective property of the capsule helps to protect and repair damaged nerves, which can help to restore normal nerve function and alleviate nerve pain symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Malkangani plant contain a high concentration of alkaloids and flavonoids, which have been shown to have potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the Malkangani plant are rich in glycosides and terpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and pain in the nervous system.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried celastrus paniculatus leaves and seeds in amounts of 20 grams each.
Grind the dried herbs into fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the powdered herb blend with 100 milliliters of pure water and let it steep for 45 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with one teaspoon of the prepared herbal extract and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ivy capsules helps with nerve pain because they contain compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active ingredients in ivy capsules, such as falcarinol and falcarindiol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals. Additionally, ivy capsules may help to relax muscles and improve blood flow, which can further contribute to reduced nerve pain. By addressing underlying inflammation and promoting relaxation, ivy capsules provide a natural solution for alleviating nerve discomfort.
remedy_properties: Acetylcholine content: Herbal ivy capsules contain high levels of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter that helps regulate muscle contractions and can alleviate symptoms of nerve damage such as numbness, tingling, and weakness.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ivy contains anti-inflammatory compounds like sesquiterpenes and triterpenoids, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected area, thereby relieving pain associated with nerve damage.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal ivy capsules possess antioxidant properties that help protect against oxidative stress and damage to nerve tissue, reducing the risk of nerve damage and alleviating symptoms of nerve pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids and saponins, Ivy roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nerve inflammation and alleviate nerve pain.
Rhyzomes: Containing compounds like dihydroactinidiolide, ivy rhyzomes have potent analgesic and antispasmodic effects, making them effective in treating nerve pain and numbness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hedera helix leaves from your garden or purchase dried leaves from a reputable supplier.
Dry the leaves in a low-temperature oven set at 150°f for 2 hours to preserve their active compounds.
Combine 1 part dried hedera helix leaves with 1/2 part cornstarch and grind into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered hedera helix blend with 500 mg of vitamin b12 and 250 mg of omega-3 fatty acids in a small bowl, taking approximately 10 minutes to prepare the mixture.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with 500 mg of the herbal blend and store them in an airtight container for up to 6 months, allowing for daily dosing as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Jesuit's bark capsules helps with nerve pain because it contains a bioactive compound called hyperforin, which has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. When ingested, hyperforin works to reduce inflammation in the nervous system, calming irritated nerves and promoting healing. Additionally, Jesuit's bark has been shown to increase the body's natural production of neurotransmitters, such as serotonin and dopamine, which play a crucial role in pain modulation. This unique combination of mechanisms helps to alleviate nerve pain, providing relief for individuals suffering from conditions such as fibromyalgia, peripheral neuropathy, and diabetic nerve damage.
remedy_properties: Analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects: The bioactive compounds in Jesuit's bark capsules have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with nerve damage or compression.

Neuroprotective effects: The alkaloids present in Jesuit's bark have been shown to possess neuroprotective properties, which may help to prevent the degeneration of neurons and promote the survival of nerve cells, reducing the risk of chronic nerve pain.
Antioxidant activity: Jesuit's bark contains antioxidants that can help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which is often associated with nerve damage and neuropathic pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain high levels of quinine, a key component in reducing inflammation and relieving nerve pain.
Barks: The barks contain cinchonine, a compound that has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in managing nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cinchona officinalis bark and dry it completely using air circulation or shade drying for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder and sift through a #20 mesh sieve to remove any lumps.
Mix 1 part of the powdered cinchona officinalis with 5 parts of tapioca starch in a small bowl, adding 10ml of filtered water gradually until a smooth paste forms.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with approximately 0.25 grams of the powder-tapioca-starch mixture and seal them immediately to prevent moisture exposure.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight glass container at room temperature for up to 6 months, ensuring they are kept away from direct sunlight and moisture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thorow-wax capsules helps with nerve pain because they contain a blend of natural herbs that work synergistically to alleviate discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties in these capsules reduce swelling and irritation around the affected nerves, while the analgesic ingredients provide relief from chronic pain. Additionally, the herbal extracts help to improve blood circulation, which aids in the delivery of essential nutrients and oxygen to the damaged nerve endings, promoting healing and regeneration.
remedy_properties: Myristicin: This compound has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce nerve pain by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body.

Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene oxide has potent antioxidant properties, which can help protect nerves from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, reducing inflammation and pain.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in Herbal Thorow-Wax Capsules have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm nervous activity and reduce anxiety, stress, and pain associated with nerve issues.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of the plant contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds responsible for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots of Thorow-wax have been traditionally used to treat various types of pain, including nerve-related pain due to their ability to reduce inflammation and relieve discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh bupleurum chinense roots and dry them thoroughly in shade for 7-10 days or using low heat.
Grind dried bupleurum chinense roots into fine powder using mortar and pestle or coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with 200mg of gelatin and 100mg of glycerin in a bowl for 3 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them tightly to ensure freshness.
Store the herbal bupleurum chinense capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with nerve pain because they contain compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the nerves. The glycosides present in licorice root also have analgesic and anxiolytic effects, providing relief from pain, numbness, and tingling sensations often associated with nerve damage. Additionally, licorice capsules can help to restore balance to the nervous system by reducing oxidative stress and promoting antioxidant activity, ultimately alleviating symptoms of neuropathic pain.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and ease pain in the nerves.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice root, such as liquiritin and isoliquiritigenin, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help protect nerves from damage and reduce oxidative stress.
Triterpenoid saponins: These compounds have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate nerve pain by reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate nerve pain.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain compounds like isoliquiritigenin and licoricidin, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective for treating nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of dried licorice root powder from glycyrrhiza uralensis plant and sift through a fine-mesh sieve.
Weigh out 150 milliliters of glycerin bp and add it to the powdered licorice root in a clean glass container.
Mix the powders and glycerin together for exactly 10 minutes using a stainless steel spatula to ensure uniform blending.
Fill hard gelatin capsules with precisely 500 milligrams of the prepared mixture, sealing each capsule carefully to prevent contamination.
Label and store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years or longer if stored properly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime capsules helps with nerve pain because they contain natural herbs that work to calm and soothe the nervous system. The anti-inflammatory properties of these herbs help reduce swelling in the nerves, which can contribute to pain and discomfort. Additionally, the antioxidants present in lime capsules help to protect the nerves from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy nerve function and reducing the sensation of pain. This natural approach can provide long-lasting relief from nerve pain without the need for harsh medications.
remedy_properties: Limonene: Limonene has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce nerve pain by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body.
Vitamin C: Vitamin C is a powerful antioxidant that helps to protect the nerves from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to nerve pain.
Citral: Citral has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help to reduce swelling and pain in the nerves, promoting healing and relief from chronic nerve pain conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, the roots of the lime plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate nerve pain.
Rhyzomes: The underground stems of the lime plant contain volatile oils that can help reduce inflammation and numb nerve endings, providing relief from nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of tilia x europaea flowers at dawn to ensure highest potency and quality.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves in a warm air circulation oven at 50°c for 30 minutes to remove excess moisture.
Combine 500mg of dried tilia x europaea leaves with 1000mg of silica gel as filler material and grind into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Fill hard-shell capsules with the powdered mixture to a dosage of 250mg per capsule, ensuring accurate filling without overfilling or underfilling.
Store the filled capsules in airtight glass containers at room temperature away from direct sunlight and moisture for up to 6 months before use.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to nerve pain that are treated with herbal capsules are arthritis, fibromyalgia, and peripheral neuropathy. Capsules containing herbs such as turmeric, ginger, and cayenne pepper can help alleviate symptoms of joint inflammation, muscle soreness, and numbness. Additionally, herbal formulas featuring ashwagandha, passionflower, and valerian root may also be used to calm nerve irritation and promote relaxation.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with nerve pain is Turmeric/Curcumin, Ashwagandha, and Ginkgo Biloba. Turmeric/Curcumin contains anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and alleviate pain, while Ashwagandha helps to calm the nervous system and reduce stress. Ginkgo Biloba, with its antioxidant properties, improves blood flow to the affected areas, promoting healing and reducing nerve inflammation. Taking these herbal capsules together may provide significant relief from nerve pain, promoting overall well-being and quality of life. Consult a healthcare professional before adding any supplements to your routine.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Reviews in the neurosciences, natural products, including herbal capsules, have been used for a long time to manage various neurological problems, including neuropathies. The study involved a review of 3679 papers, from which 22 studies were included that clinically evaluated the effects of medicinal plants in neuropathy, including diabetic neuropathy, chemotherapy-induced peripheral neuropathy, carpal tunnel syndrome, and HIV-associated neuropathy. The results showed that some herbal capsules, such as Cannabis sativa (hemp), Linum usitatissimum (linseed oil), capsaicin, and Goshajinkigan, demonstrated clinical efficacy in improving pain, nerve function, nerve conduction velocity, and quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with nerve pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compound, gingerol, in these capsules reduces inflammation and blocks the production of pain-causing chemicals. As a result, it provides effective relief from nerve pain, often associated with conditions like sciatica, fibromyalgia, and neuropathy. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to protect nerve cells from damage, further contributing to its pain-relieving effects. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective alternative to manage nerve pain.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce nerve pain by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help to alleviate nerve pain by inhibiting the activity of pain-sensing nerves.

Curcumenol: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce nerve pain by preventing the damage caused by free radicals and reducing inflammation in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are also used for nerve pain capsules due to their rich content of bioactive compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation.

Stems: The stems of Zingiber officinale are occasionally used in nerve pain capsules, although they contain lower levels of active compounds compared to the rhizome and roots, they still contribute to the overall analgesic effect.
remedy_recipe: Harvest zingiber officinale rhizomes from mature plants after the foliage has begun to yellow for 2-3 weeks.
Clean and dry the rhizomes in a warm place with good air circulation for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a mill or coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with a carrier agent such as potato starch in a 1:4 ratio by weight.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal powder mixture using a capsule filler.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with nerve pain because they contain antioxidants and flavonoids that improve blood flow to the brain and nerves. This increased blood flow enhances the delivery of oxygen and nutrients, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginkgo biloba also help to calm irritated nerve endings, providing relief from conditions such as sciatica, peripheral neuropathy, and other types of nerve pain. Regular use of ginkgo biloba capsules may promote nerve health and reduce discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with nerve pain by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the affected areas, which can help alleviate pain.
Bilobalide: It helps with nerve pain by acting as an antioxidant and reducing oxidative stress, which can contribute to nerve damage and pain.
Ginkgolides: They help with nerve pain by inhibiting platelet-activating factor (PAF), a substance that can cause inflammation and pain in the nervous system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to provide neuroprotective effects.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds contain a compound called ginkgolide, which is thought to help alleviate nerve pain and improve blood flow to the brain.
Buds: Ginkgo buds are sometimes used, as they are believed to have a higher concentration of active compounds than leaves, although more research is needed to confirm their effectiveness.
remedy_recipe: Dry 200g of ginkgo biloba leaves in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 2 hours to preserve their potency.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder at 20,000 rpm for 1 minute.
Mix 10g of the powdered ginkgo biloba with 20g of a filler material like rice flour in a bowl.
Encapsulate the mixture in 100 empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filler machine in 5 minutes.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum capsules helps with nerve pain because of its rich content of capsaicin, a potent compound that targets nerve endings and blocks the production of a chemical called substance P. This leads to a reduction in the transmission of pain signals to the brain, providing relief from nerve pain. Capsaicin also increases the levels of neurotransmitters like serotonin and dopamine, which help to alleviate pain and promote a sense of well-being. This natural approach to pain management has been used for centuries to treat various types of nerve pain.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin helps with nerve pain by inhibiting the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain.
Capsaicinoids: Capsaicinoids, specifically capsaicin, help with nerve pain by blocking the action of sodium channels in nerve cells, reducing the transmission of pain signals.
Ferulic acid: Ferulic acid helps with nerve pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing oxidative stress, which contribute to nerve pain.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Capsicum annuum are commonly used to make capsules for nerve pain due to their high capsaicin content, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Capsicum annuum are used to make capsules for nerve pain as they contain capsaicinoids, particularly capsaicin, which helps in reducing pain and inflammation.

Fruits: The fruits of Capsicum annuum, particularly the ripened peppers, are used to make capsules for nerve pain due to their high concentration of capsaicin, which has potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried capsicum annuum fruits at the ripe red stage for optimal potency.
Dry the capsicum annuum fruits further in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 24 hours.
Grind 150g of dried capsicum annuum fruits into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the capsicum annuum powder with 10g of gelatin and 10g of glycerin in a mortar.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal immediately to preserve potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis capsules helps with nerve pain because they contain valerenic acid, a natural compound with potent anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties. This unique combination helps to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated nerve endings, leading to a decrease in pain sensitivity. Additionally, the calming effects of Valeriana officinalis help to regulate the body's stress response, further alleviating nerve pain caused by anxiety and stress-related factors. This natural remedy promotes relaxation and reduces discomfort, providing relief from nerve pain.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: It helps with nerve pain by inhibiting the activity of neurotransmitters that transmit pain signals in the nervous system.
Valeranone: It has sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its analgesic and anti-nociceptive effects in nerve pain management.
Isovaleric acid: It has a sedative effect on the nervous system, reducing excitability and inflammation, which helps to alleviate nerve pain and promote relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in valerenic acid, which is responsible for the plant's sedative and pain-relieving properties.
Rhyzomes: Similar to roots, but contain a higher concentration of valerenic acid, making them a preferred choice for nerve pain relief.
Seeds: Contain a compound called isovaleramide, which has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots at dawn when the plant is at its highest moisture content and store in a cool dry place.
Dry the roots in a single layer at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours or until they reach a moisture level of 8.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder with a fine mesh screen.
Mix one part of the powdered root with one part of gelatin and one part of water in a small bowl to create a thick paste.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the paste and seal immediately to preserve the potency and shelf life of the capsules.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata capsules helps with nerve pain because they contain a unique blend of bioactive compounds that work synergistically to calm and soothe the nervous system. The flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides present in the herb have a sedative and anti-inflammatory effect, reducing pain and discomfort associated with nerve damage. By modulating the body's response to pain, Passiflora incarnata capsules provide relief from symptoms such as tingling, numbness, and burning sensations, allowing individuals to regain control over their lives.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce nerve pain and inflammation associated with conditions like neuropathy.
Alkaloids (specifically, harman alkaloids): These compounds have a sedative effect, which can help calm the nervous system and reduce pain sensitivity, providing relief from nerve pain.

Flavonoid glycosides (specifically, vitexin): These compounds have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce nerve pain and promote healing in affected areas.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Passiflora incarnata are used to make capsules for nerve pain due to their high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Passiflora incarnata are used to make capsules for nerve pain due to their high content of flavonoids and apigenin, which have a calming effect on the nervous system.

Fruits: The fruits of Passiflora incarnata are used to make capsules for nerve pain due to their high concentration of flavonoids, particularly passifloric acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried aerial parts of the plant at peak flowering time for optimal potency.
Clean and dry the aerial parts in a low-temperature oven at 150f for 2 hours.
Grind the dried aerial parts into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the powder with 200g of gelatin and 100g of glycerin to fill empty gelatin capsules.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa capsules helps with nerve pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound, curcumin, blocks the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, reducing inflammation and discomfort in the nerves. Additionally, its antioxidant properties neutralize free radicals that can damage nerve cells, promoting healing and regeneration. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Curcuma longa capsules provide relief from nerve pain, improving overall nerve function and quality of life for those suffering from conditions such as neuropathy and sciatica.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps with nerve pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which contribute to the development of pain and inflammation.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): It helps with nerve pain by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and blocking the activity of pain-sensing receptors, leading to a decrease in pain perception.

Bis-demethoxycurcumin (BDMC): It helps with nerve pain by inhibiting the activation of pain-sensing receptors and reducing the production of pro-inflammatory molecules, which contribute to the development of pain and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Curcuma longa for nerve pain capsules due to their high concentration of curcuminoids, which provide anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Roots: Roots are also a popular choice for nerve pain capsules as they contain a significant amount of curcumin, a powerful compound that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
Leaves: Leaves are sometimes used to make nerve pain capsules due to their lower cost and similar curcumin content to rhyzomes and roots, although they may not be as effective.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried curcuma longa root and sift it through a fine-mesh sieve.
Weigh and mix 250g of gelatin and 50g of water in a heat-proof bowl.
Add 50g of the sifted curcuma longa root powder to the gelatin mixture and stir well.
Heat the gelatin mixture in a double boiler or a bain-marie at 80-90°c for 10-15 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the curcuma longa gel mixture and seal them immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cannabis sativa capsules helps with nerve pain because they contain cannabinoids, specifically THC and CBD, which interact with the body's endocannabinoid system. This interaction helps reduce inflammation and modulate pain signals in the nervous system. The analgesic properties of cannabis sativa also help block the release of pain-causing chemicals, such as substance P. As a result, nerve pain, often caused by conditions like multiple sclerosis, diabetes, and shingles, can be effectively managed with the use of these capsules.
remedy_constituents: Cannabidiol (CBD): Helps with nerve pain by reducing inflammation and modulating pain perception through its interaction with the body's endocannabinoid system.
Tetrahydrocannabinol (THC): Helps with nerve pain by activating the body's cannabinoid receptors, which can lead to the release of natural pain-relieving chemicals and a reduction in pain perception.
Beta-caryophyllene: Helps with nerve pain by acting as a CB2 receptor agonist, which can reduce inflammation and modulate pain perception in the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their high concentration of CBD (cannabidiol), which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties without the psychoactive effects of THC.
Seeds: They contain a high amount of CBD, along with other beneficial compounds, and are often used as a supplement to help manage nerve pain and inflammation.
Buds: They are high in THC (tetrahydrocannabinol), which can help with pain relief, but they are often used in combination with CBD to create a balanced effect for nerve pain management.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of cannabis sativa flowers and leaves when the flowers are in full bloom and the leaves are a vibrant green color.
Dry the harvested cannabis sativa in a single layer on paper towels for 7 to 10 days in a well-ventilated area.
Grind 30g of dried cannabis sativa into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the ground cannabis sativa with 200g of carrier oil such as coconut oil in a double boiler on low heat for 30 minutes.
Fill 100 capsules with 0.5g of the cannabis sativa oil mixture each and seal immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera capsules helps with nerve pain because they contain a potent adaptogenic herb called Ashwagandha. The active compounds in Ashwagandha, such as withanolides, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system. This can lead to a reduction in nerve pain and discomfort. Additionally, Ashwagandha has been shown to improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted by nerve pain, and promote relaxation, further alleviating symptoms of nerve pain.
remedy_constituents: Withanone: Withanone has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with nerve pain.
Withaferin A: Withaferin A has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which help alleviate nerve pain by reducing inflammation and blocking pain signals.
Withanolides: Withanolides have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect nerve cells from damage and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating nerve pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: As the primary source of bioactive compounds, roots are used to create capsules that help alleviate nerve pain due to their high concentration of withanolides.
Leaves: Rich in withanolides and other beneficial compounds, leaves are used to make capsules that provide relief from nerve pain and inflammation.
Seeds: Containing a unique combination of withanolides and other nutrients, seeds are used to create capsules that support nerve health and reduce pain.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250 grams of dried withania somnifera root and store it in an airtight container.
Grind the dried root into fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder for 5 minutes.
Sift the powder through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any lumps or large particles for 10 minutes.
Fill a gelatin capsule with 250 milligrams of the sifted powder and seal it immediately.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Acmella oleracea capsules helps with nerve pain because they contain compounds like spilanthol, which have potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds work by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body, thereby reducing inflammation and discomfort. Additionally, Acmella oleracea has been found to have a local anesthetic effect, numbing the area around the nerve and providing relief from pain. This natural approach to pain management has been shown to be effective in alleviating nerve pain and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Spilanthol: This sesquiterpene acts as a local anesthetic, numbing the affected nerve area and providing relief from pain.
Essential oils (mainly sesquiterpene lactones): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce swelling and alleviate nerve pain.
Phenolic compounds (e.g., flavonoids and phenolic acids): These antioxidants may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, contributing to the relief of nerve pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are a primary source for making capsules for nerve pain due to their high content of spilanthol, a compound with potent analgesic properties.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used to make capsules for nerve pain as they contain a high concentration of spilanthol and other bioactive compounds that help alleviate nerve discomfort.
Stems: Stems are utilized to make capsules for nerve pain as they contain a significant amount of spilanthol, which has been shown to provide relief from nerve-related pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of acmella oleracea leaves and flowers that are free of contaminants and debris.
Dry the harvested acmella oleracea in a low-temperature dehydrator for 2-3 hours at 50c temperature.
Powder 50g of dried acmella oleracea in a coffee grinder for 10-15 seconds or until finely powdered.
Mix 1g of the powdered acmella oleracea with 1g of tapioca starch and 1g of gelatin to create a capsule filling.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the acmella oleracea mixture and seal them securely for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum capsules helps with nerve pain because they contain a high concentration of the active compound hypericin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate nerve pain, numbness, and tingling sensations associated with conditions like sciatica, shingles, and neuropathy. The capsules work by reducing inflammation, blocking pain signals, and promoting nerve regeneration, providing fast and effective relief from nerve pain.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Acts as a potent anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective agent, helping to reduce nerve pain and inflammation.
Naphthodianthrones (mainly Hypericin): Exhibits anti-neuropathic and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its pain-relieving effects on nerves.
Flavonoids (mainly Quercetin): Possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to alleviate nerve pain and oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their flavonoid and xanthone content, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Utilized for their high concentration of hyperforin and hypericin, which have neuroprotective and pain-relieving effects.
Seeds: Contain compounds that support the plant's medicinal properties, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves at the optimal time when they are in full bloom for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested hypericum perforatum plant material in a warm place for 7-10 days to remove moisture.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for uniform consistency.
Fill empty vegetable gelatin capsules with the powdered hypericum perforatum plant material in the amount of 1:5 ratio.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.
